Name: obol
Note: dialect form of word for 'nail' -- nails were used as currency, with 6 nails being able to be held in the hand (which is what δραχμή means)

Name: gramma
Note: 1/24 of an ounce, unsure of relation to normal meaning of term to do with writing

Name: holce
Note: general term for weight, identified with common unit (as pound in british)

Name: oungia
Note: Lat word adopted by sicilian Greeks

Name: tetarton
Note: a fourth part of a litra

Name: mna
Note: from ancient semitic root 'to count'; equals 1/60 of a talent and 60 shekels; for greeks, equaled 70 then 100 drachmai, varied in weight, this is attic

Name: mystron
Note: named for a piece of bread scooped out used as a spoon

Name: choinix
Note: name for the nave (center part) of a wheeel, a person's daily measure of grain, 4 cups

Name: chous
Note: something you pour out of; 12 cups

Name: ceramion
Note: extended from κέραμος, a kind of clay, used mostly for κεραμίς or tiles
